text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers review each other’s code, they can catch bugs early, before the code reaches production. This saves time and money, as fixing issues later is much more difficult. Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews promote knowledge sharing. Team members learn from different approaches, new techniques, and better ways to solve problems. This helps everyone grow as developers. Over time, consistent code reviews improve the overall quality of the team's work. The code becomes cleaner, more maintainable, and easier to understand. In short, code reviews are not just about checking errors—they are a valuable tool for building a stronger, smarter team.
